title: Onboarding takes too long
description_normalized: New engineers require 3-4 weeks before shipping their first PR due to scattered documentation across Notion, Confluence, and tribal knowledge.
impact_summary: Every new hire loses 2+ weeks of productivity; compounds with team growth.

title: Deploy pipeline is flaky
description_normalized: CI/CD pipeline fails approximately 20% of the time with intermittent timeout errors, forcing teams to retry deployments and waste engineering time.
impact_summary: Daily productivity loss across all engineers; erodes trust in deployment process.

title: Customer feedback never reaches engineering
description_normalized: Product and support teams collect customer feedback but there is no systematic process to route it to engineering. Pain points are discovered months after they're reported.
impact_summary: Engineering builds without customer context; misaligned priorities and rework.

title: Too many meetings, no decisions
description_normalized: Team has 6+ recurring meetings per week but decisions are rarely documented or followed up on. Attendees leave unclear on next steps and action items.
impact_summary: Hours lost weekly in unproductive meetings; decisions get relitigated.

title: No visibility into what other teams are working on
description_normalized: Each squad works in isolation with no shared view of priorities. Duplicated effort and conflicting priorities are discovered too late to course-correct.
impact_summary: Wasted effort from duplication; missed opportunities for collaboration.

title: Incident response is ad hoc
description_normalized: No runbooks or clear escalation paths exist for production incidents. The person who notices an alert handles it alone with no structured process.
impact_summary: Incidents take longer to resolve; burnout on individuals; risk of cascading failures.

title: Knowledge lost when people leave
description_normalized: When employees leave, critical context about systems, architecture decisions, and historical reasoning disappears. No handoff process or knowledge capture exists.
impact_summary: Institutional knowledge erodes with each departure; recovery cost is high.

title: Sprint planning is guesswork
description_normalized: Story estimation relies on gut feel rather than data. Velocity is unpredictable and sprint goals are missed by 30-40% regularly.
impact_summary: Unreliable delivery commitments; erodes stakeholder trust.

title: Support tickets pile up with no owner
description_normalized: Tier 2 support tickets requiring engineering input sit in a queue with no clear triage process or ownership. Average response time is 5 business days.
impact_summary: Customer satisfaction drops; support team frustrated; churn risk increases.

title: No shared understanding of technical debt
description_normalized: Everyone acknowledges technical debt exists but there is no inventory, prioritization framework, or dedicated time to address it. Debt grows unchecked.
impact_summary: Velocity decreases over time; increasing fragility and maintenance burden.
